description_long: This barbecue grill features a rectangular, open-frame design constructed from dark metal, with a lattice-patterned grilling surface. The grill is supported by four sturdy, angled legs, providing stability and elevating the cooking surface above the ground. The lattice grate allows for even heat distribution and is suitable for charcoal grilling, as there is no visible gas or electric component. The structure is robust and industrial, with no lid or side shelves, emphasizing a minimalist, utilitarian aesthetic. The open design facilitates airflow beneath the grate, ideal for traditional charcoal grilling. The overall appearance suggests durability and suitability for public parks or communal outdoor spaces.
description: A rectangular, open-frame metal barbecue grill with a lattice grate and four angled legs, designed for charcoal grilling. It has a minimalist, industrial look with no lid or side shelves.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rectangular frame and lattice grate, with two legs visible at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the full length of the grill, the lattice pattern, and the angled legs on both sides.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, showing the rectangular frame and lattice grate with two supporting legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, displaying the grill's length and angled legs.
